text: Excuse me, sir. Sir, I have a question for you. How old were you when you became a millionaire? I've seen you before. You're Miles Gehrer, right? I am. Can I get one minute with you real quick? Sure. So how did you get rich for the people that don't know? I played defensive end for the Cleveland Browns. You're one of the greatest defensive end of all time. If you say so. Tell the people what records you broke this year. For those that don't know, man. Single season sack record. The most of anybody in a single season. Yes, sir. What was the most amount of money you made in a single year? I can't say that. More than 10 million? Yes. More than 15 million? Yes. More than 20 million? Yes. More than 20 million dollars, man. How does that feel? Did you come from a lot of money? No. Was that one of your driving factors? Was that motivation for you? Absolutely. I wanted to give my family a better life, especially my grand. She passed me right before. I got into college and always tried to name down on my tape. When a lot of athletes go into money, they're not used to seeing that much money and a lot of them go and blow it. What are you doing to build that generational wealth? Teams and energy. Renovable energy. Energy. Yes, sir. Part owner and the cabs. Are you a part of a basketball team? I am. How old are you? 30. 30 years old. 30 years old. I'm going to challenge you. What's the greatest piece of advice you received throughout your career? In high school, that's when it was, I was really into basketball and my head coach at the time for football, they told me I needed to be all in or all out. You can't do anything half-assed. If I wanted to be great, I had to give everything to it all. Did you ever fear anything on the football field? Never. Why not? They put on the shorts just like me and they got to see me just like I see them. What would yourself talk like? Because you see you, man. I mean, you are an aggressor. You get after it. What are you telling yourself mentally when you're on that football field? You're prepared for everything that you're going to see. You're the greatest in the world. You know, it's time to go out and show it. Did you ever worry about what people thought about you? Never. Why not? I'm sure you've had haters with this amount of success. Yeah, I mean, you are who you are. And Jalen Brown told me that they don't build statutes for haters. They build them for people that do things like miles of gear. You damn right. Do you believe in God? I do. You know, every sack I put up to this guy, I kiss my wrist and I have a cross there as well and I believe that's almost completely part of my process and my success. Jesus is keen. Every day. You've got amazing advice, my friend. Thank you so much. I got to ask you, man. I connect with all the millionaires and billionaires that you do. And could I get your contact? Yeah.
